paper_metadata.title: HTLM: Hyper-Text Pre-Training and Prompting of Language Models
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce HTLM, a hyper-text language model trained on a large-scale web crawl. Modeling hyper-text has a number of advantages: (1) it is easily gathered at scale, (2) it provides rich document-level and end-task-adjacent supervision (e.g. class and id attributes often encode document category information), and (3) it allows for new structured prompting that follows the established semantics of HTML (e.g. to do zero-shot summarization by infilling title tags for a webpage that contains the input text). We show that pretraining with a BART-style denoising loss directly on simplified HTML provides highly effective transfer for a wide range of end tasks and supervision levels. HTLM matches or exceeds the performance of comparably sized text-only LMs for zero-shot prompting and fine-tuning for classification benchmarks, while also setting new state-of-the-art performance levels for zero-shot summarization. We also find that hyper-text prompts provide more value to HTLM, in terms of data efficiency, than plain text prompts do for existing LMs, and that HTLM is highly effective at auto-prompting itself, by simply generating the most likely hyper-text formatting for any available training data. We will release all code and models to support future HTLM research.

extraction_data.innovation: HTLM-R3F applies the R3F fine-tuning method to HTLM, improving performance on the GLUE benchmark by leveraging structured prompts and robust fine-tuning techniques.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large subset of the internet (HTML data)
extraction_data.application: Fine-tuning for language understanding, classification, and natural language generation
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving fine-tuning performance on language understanding tasks using structured prompts and robust fine-tuning methods
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with HTML-based prompting
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Fine-tuning with R3F method and structured prompts
extraction_data.extension: Size hint scheme for masked spans to provide noisy hints about the length of the masked text, enabling finer-grained task-specific length priors during prompting.
extraction_data.paper_title: HTLM: Hyper-Text Pre-Training and Prompting of Language Models

extraction_data.innovation: HTLM introduces hyper-text pre-training, enabling the model to leverage HTML structure for improved prompting and generation. It uses size hints and structured prompts to control output length and style, differing from prior work by incorporating markup language context directly into pre-training.
extraction_data.application: Summarization, classification, natural language generation, table-to-text generation
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving language model prompting and generation using structured hyper-text data
extraction_data.extension: Size hints and structured HTML-based prompting enable fine-grained control over generation length and style.
extraction_data.paper_title: HTLM: Hyper-Text Pre-Training and Prompting of Language Models

extraction_data.innovation: HTLM-Manual-NS is a variant of HTLM using manually engineered prompts without size hints for generation tasks.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving language model prompting and generation using structured hyper-text data
extraction_data.paper_title: HTLM: Hyper-Text Pre-Training and Prompting of Language Models

extraction_data.innovation: HTLM-Manual-S is a variant of HTLM using manually engineered prompts with size hints for generation tasks, enabling better control over output length and complexity.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving language model prompting and generation using structured hyper-text data
extraction_data.extension: Size hints policy for controlling generation length and style.
extraction_data.paper_title: HTLM: Hyper-Text Pre-Training and Prompting of Language Models

extraction_data.innovation: HTLM-Auto-S is a variant of HTLM using size hints-based auto-prompting for generation tasks, outperforming prior state-of-the-art models in zero-shot summarization.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving language model prompting and generation using structured hyper-text data
extraction_data.extension: Size hints-based auto-prompting for improved generation control.
extraction_data.paper_title: HTLM: Hyper-Text Pre-Training and Prompting of Language Models
